extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won sprint from mid-pack at 200m, outmaneuvering GAVIRIA who misjudged positioning after Quick-Step controlled final 2km
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled final 2km for GAVIRIA but mid-pack positioning forced sprinter to waste energy bridging instead of launching
GROENEWEGEN positioned behind Belgian champion RICKAERT at 1km banner, using third wheel to avoid wind before late move center
Dimension Data lost CAVENDISH before 3km despite controlling front, revealing lead-out train collapsed too early on downhill section
SAGAN boxed behind GAVIRIA inside 1km after following Quick-Step's acceleration, tactical error riding reactive instead of positioning independently

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: read GAVIRIA's mid-pack trap correctly and attacked center lane at 200m for 10th win of season
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Quick-Step's front control forced him 15 positions back at 2km, misjudged energy required to bridge uphill
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: lost Dimension Data train before 3km but 10th place shows improving form after recent drought
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: followed GAVIRIA's wheel reactively instead of independent positioning, boxed at crucial 500m acceleration point
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: not even top-30 after switching from Quick-Step, confidence visibly declining without dedicated lead-out infrastructure
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: Bahrain-Merida brought only HAUSSLER as support, revealing team not committed to bunch sprint investment
